title: cistus × escartianus nothosubsp. escartianus
intro: Cistus × escartianus nothosubsp. escartianus, also known as the Escart's rockrose, is a hybrid plant species within the Cistus genus, characterized by its white or pale pink flowers and grey-green foliage. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The Escart's rockrose has been associated with various health benefits, such as soothing skin irritations, reducing inflammation, and exhibiting antioxidant properties, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Cistus × escartianus nothosubsp. escartianus include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal benefits.
